message:
perf(verify): fast-fail on file size mismatch before SHA256 hash

Add an early size check in check_integrity() that compares
fs::metadata().len() against the expected entry.size before computing
the expensive SHA256 hash. For tampered or corrupted files where the
size alone differs, this avoids reading and hashing the entire file
contents — a significant speedup for large bundles.

- Uses canonical path (via fs::metadata on the resolved path) so
  symlinks resolve to the actual target size
- Updates test assertion to accept either "Size mismatch" or
  "Hash mismatch" since the size check now fires first when both
  size and content differ

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
